Petition For An Order Decreasing The Bond And Requesting An Allowance On The Compensation Of The Attorney Of The Personal Representative

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Filing Either A Petition For Order For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration Pursuant To Prob. Code Sec. 12200 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Make A Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-22-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-29-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Kevin Jentes, Esq., for Douglas Lambert, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court advises that it has determined that probate code sec. 6124 presumption has been overcome. The court and counsel discuss whether his client is bondable. Counsel advises that he has confirmed a bond company that is willing to issue a bond. The court and counsel then discuss an appropriate bond amount. After discussion, the court determines that a bond in the amount of $5,050,000 shall be ordered. Counsel is admonished to file a petition to increase the bond after marshaling assets, if needed.
The court confirms that there are no appearances to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The Lost will dated Oct-11-2015 is admitted to probate. Douglas Lambert is appointed Executor of the estate with limited authority. Bond is set at $5,050,000. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court.

Status hearing. The Personal Representative shall file either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code Sec.12200 et seq. two weeks before the hearing date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
